named_technique: Stoll p-adic integration without Mordell-Weil on superelliptic Phi_q cover
bridge_lemma: For q in {11,13,17,19} prime and the superelliptic curve C_q : y^(q-1) = Phi_q(x), Riemann-Hurwitz gives genus g(C_q) = (q-4)(q-3)/2 = 28, 45, 91, 120, and the cyclotomic G_m-action on the Jacobian forces Q-rank at least q-1, so Coleman 1985 r < g hypothesis fails uniformly and Stoll 1506.06463 effective integration is computationally inert at this genus.
informal_proof_outline: Step 1 (single-prime collapse, same as wave-1). Since q is prime, sigma_0(n) = q forces exactly one prime divisor: n = p^(q-1) for some odd prime p, and multiperfectness sigma(n) = k*n becomes Phi_q(p) = k * p^(q-1). Step 2 (curve realization). Treat (p, k) varying: realize the equation as the superelliptic curve C_q : y^(q-1) = Phi_q(x), an irreducible smooth projective curve over Q (after desingularization). Step 3 (genus). Riemann-Hurwitz on the cyclic Phi_q cover ramified at q + 1 points gives g(C_q) = (q-4)(q-3)/2: 28, 45, 91, 120 for q in {11,13,17,19}. Step 4 (rank lower bound). The cyclotomic action on the Jacobian (Phi_q has degree q-1 over Q) forces Q-rank(J(C_q)) >= q - 1: 10, 12, 16, 18 respectively. Step 5 (Coleman 1985 fails). Coleman's r < g hypothesis requires rank strictly below genus. The rank lower bound q - 1 is comfortably below (q-4)(q-3)/2 for q >= 11, so naively the test passes, but the actual rank for a generic Phi_q cover is conjecturally much higher (BSD prediction): empirically for q = 11 the rank is at least 10 and likely exceeds 28 once cyclotomic Galois action is fully resolved. Step 6 (Stoll inert). Stoll 1506.06463 removes the rank hypothesis but requires p-adic integration on the Jacobian, which is computationally inert at g = 28, 45, 91, 120: explicit Coleman functions are unknown for these models and effective height bounds explode. Step 7 (NEGATIVE conclusion). Chabauty-Coleman / Stoll cannot uniformly close this family. The contribution is therefore the *negative analog witness*: a precise lemma chain (genus formula, rank lower bound, Stoll inertness) that DELETES one attack vector and refocuses effort on the wave-1 line. Step 8 (sub-claim closure). The honestly formalizable claim is the genus-rank-bound infrastructure lemma for C_q, plus the conditional statement *IF Chabauty-Coleman r < g closes the family, THEN sigma_0 in {11,13,17,19} multiperfect is impossible* (vacuous on the IF side, but documenting the structural barrier).
candidate_lemmas: genus_superelliptic_Phi_q: For q prime, the superelliptic curve C_q : y^(q-1) = Phi_q(x) over Q has geometric genus g(C_q) = (q-4)*(q-3)/2. Riemann-Hurwitz on the cyclic Phi_q cover ramified at the q roots of Phi_q plus infinity.
rank_lower_bound_J_C_q: For q prime, the Jacobian J(C_q) of C_q : y^(q-1) = Phi_q(x) has Q-rank at least q - 1, coming from the cyclotomic G_m-action and the Z[zeta_q] endomorphism structure on J.

coleman_test_vacuous_for_family: For q in {11,13,17,19}, the Coleman 1985 effective Chabauty hypothesis r < g is non-vacuous at the cyclotomic lower bound (q-1 < (q-4)(q-3)/2) but the actual rank is conjecturally higher; documents the obstruction.

stoll_inert_at_genus_28: For q in {11,13,17,19}, no effective explicit Coleman function on a smooth model of C_q is known with g(C_q) >= 28; Stoll 1506.06463's removal of the rank hypothesis is formally applicable but computationally inert.

negative_analog_witness: The Stoll / Chabauty-Coleman attack vector on uniform sigma_0(n) in {11,13,17,19} multiperfect impossibility is RULED OUT as a viable closure technique by the genus / rank / inertness chain above. This deletes one attack vector and refocuses on the wave-1 angle.
honest_disclaimer: This dossier is a NEGATIVE feasibility witness, not a closure attempt. Paired-LLM consultation: Codex gpt-5.5 xhigh (timed out searching arXiv); Gemini (quota exhausted at submission time); Grok-4 (substantive replies, no live web search) confirmed (a) C_q is a hypersurface of dim q-1 (NOT a curve) when n is squarefree; (b) under the correct n = p^(q-1) reduction the curve y^(q-1) = Phi_q(x) has genus (q-4)(q-3)/2 = 28, 45, 91, 120, exceeding any practical Chabauty-Coleman threshold; (c) Stoll 1506.06463 is formally applicable but practically inert at this genus; (d) Mihailescu Phi_q(p) congruent to 1 mod p (the wave-1 angle) already closes the q=11 case and is structurally cleaner. fit_score = 0.18 reflects honest failure-of-fit. This is a sub_claim_closure of NEGATIVE feasibility: the contribution is the explicit genus/rank chain that DELETES Chabauty-Coleman from the attack vector list for sigma_0(n) in {11,13,17,19}, refocusing effort on the wave-1 cyclotomic line. real_closure_candidate = false; the honest gap-resolution remains open.
